mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly elevating equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and persistent inflation indicate stagflationary pressures that compress equity risk premiums and suppress forward returns as real yields rise and growth expectations fall.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when housing starts fall and inflation stays above 3.0% simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production in a post-shock recovery regime expands capacity utilization and boosts near-term corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative during confirmed recovery phases, or industrial production growth fails to correlate with equity outperformance over 5-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth accelerations directly increase forward earnings estimates across cyclical sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or quarterly GDP surprises show no predictive power for 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, borrowing costs and discount rates rise sharply, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where the policy rate sustains levels above the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms labor market resilience, supporting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive payrolls growth fails to lift SPX forward returns when the unemployment rate remains anchored near 5.0% or higher.

mechanism_narrative: Falling residential investment coupled with cooling housing demand signals a broad demand shock that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite simultaneous declines in residential investment and housing starts exceeding the specified thresholds.
